audio_identity.overall_style: Orchestral impression within SNES platform constraints
distinctive_elements: Reverb and spatiality to emulate orchestral sound
Balance of instrumentation for emotional narrative impact
Composition designed to emotionally invest players in narrative before matching to areas

notes: Documentation lacks specific track-level data (tempo, keys, chord progressions, time signatures, modes, melodic characteristics, loop points) and sound effect characteristics. Only composer intent and general approach documented: Noriko Matsueda composed the score with goal of creating orchestral-style music within SNES limitations through reverb, spatiality, and balance. Teruaki Sugawara was sound engineer; Hidenori Suzuki was sound programmer. Official soundtrack published by NTT Publishing on February 25, 1996. This was Matsueda's second major video game work after co-composing Front Mission with Yoko Shimomura.
